message:
feat(commands): CLUSTER/CLIENT/ACL subcommand + error-message parity

Aligns CLUSTER/CLIENT subcommand dispatch and ACL LOG entry shape with
Redis 7 byte-for-byte, including the exact error strings upstream emits
for wrong-arity and unknown-subcommand paths.

crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs
  * Replaces `CLUSTER_UNKNOWN_SUBCOMMAND_ERROR` (a single static) with
    two typed helpers that produce upstream's actual strings:
      - `cluster_wrong_subcommand_arity(subcommand)` →
        `ERR wrong number of arguments for 'cluster|<name>' command`
      - `cluster_unknown_subcommand_error(subcommand)` →
        `ERR unknown subcommand '<name>'. Try CLUSTER HELP.`
    These match src/cluster.c in redis-7.x and replace our prior
    generic "Unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for
    'CLUSTER'." — so clients parsing on the exact string work.
  * Refactors the CLUSTER dispatcher to route per-subcommand through
    these helpers at the arity-check step, so each subcommand's
    wrong-arity error uses the correct pipe-lowercase form.
  * Drops the now-unused `crc16_slot` import from fr-store (it was
    only referenced by an obsolete CLUSTER helper removed in this
    commit).

crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs
  * Adds `client_wrong_subcommand_arity(subcommand)` analogue for
    CLIENT subcommands (CLIENT LIST, CLIENT KILL, CLIENT PAUSE,
    CLIENT UNPAUSE, CLIENT REPLY, CLIENT SETNAME, …) so we emit
    "ERR wrong number of arguments for 'client|<name>' command".
  * Hides the obsolete `CLUSTER_UNKNOWN_SUBCOMMAND_ERROR` constant
    (all call sites moved to the fr-command helpers) and gates
    `ClusterSubcommand` behind `#[cfg(test)]` — runtime no longer
    needs the enum at runtime; only tests reference it.
  * ACL LOG entry upgrade to Redis 7 shape. Old struct:
        { count, reason, context: String, username, timestamp_ms }
    New struct:
        { count, reason, context: &'static str, object: String,
          username, client_info: String, entry_id: u64,
          created_at_ms: u64, updated_at_ms: u64 }
    Matches `aclLogEntry` in Redis's acl.c. The reply now includes
    `client-info`, `entry-id`, `age-seconds`, `created-at`, and
    `updated-at` fields in addition to the existing ones.
  * Adds `acl_log_age_seconds(now_ms, timestamp_ms)` helper that
    renders age in seconds with three decimals and trims trailing
    zeros to match Redis's float formatting in the ACL LOG reply.

This is a behavior change visible to clients: error strings on
unknown/wrong-arity CLUSTER and CLIENT subcommands are now the exact
strings upstream Redis emits, and ACL LOG replies carry the full
Redis-7 field set. Clients that parsed the old shorter replies will
need no change to succeed, but ones that asserted on exact payloads
will now match upstream.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
